WORK ORDER / NO. HH-2026-001 / APPROVED

HandyHub

Local experts. On demand.

A mobile booking system that connects homeowners with verified plumbers, electricians, carpenters and masons — built as three coordinated apps sharing one job, from the moment a customer taps "book" to the moment an admin closes the file.

Product
Home Services App
Scope
3 Apps, 1 System
Platform
iOS · Android · Web
Focus
Flow & UX Design
01 — The Brief

One booking, three job files, zero friction.

A handyman marketplace has three very different people relying on the same booking: the customer who wants a plumber by tomorrow morning, the tasker who needs a clear job sheet and no surprises, and the admin who is quietly making sure the right person shows up on time.

The brief was to design all three experiences as one connected system rather than three separate products — so a booking made on the customer app becomes a job the tasker can accept in one glance, and a case the admin can track without ever picking up the phone.

The hardest part wasn't any single screen. It was keeping the system simple on the surface — a handful of taps to book a service — while staying fully efficient underneath: automatic tasker matching, self-serve rescheduling, and a memory for the tasker you liked last time.

03
Connected apps sharing one data model
18
Core screens designed across the system
04
Trade categories — plumbing, electrical, carpentry, masonry
01
Auto-assignment engine replacing manual dispatch
02 — The System

Three apps, built to hand a job off cleanly.

App 01 / Customer

Customer App

Search a trade, pick a slot, add a photo of the problem and confirm. Every booking after the first one is faster — saved addresses, rebooking a preferred tasker, and a reschedule flow that never needs a phone call.

HomeBook ServiceTrack BookingRescheduleRate & Review
App 02 / Tasker

Tasker App

A daily job list, not a chat inbox. Accept or pass on an offered job, navigate to the address, mark work in progress, and close the file with photos the customer approves before earnings update.

DashboardJob DetailsAccept JobWork SummaryEarnings
App 03 / Admin

Admin Portal (RWA)

The control room. Every new booking is checked against tasker availability and auto-recommended a match — the admin only steps in to override, reassign, or update the service database by hand.

BookingsAssignment QueueTaskersCustomersReports
03 — System Schematics

How a single booking moves through the system.

Three flows, mapped end to end before a single screen was drawn — so a decision made in one app (like a tasker declining a job) always has a clear next step in the other two.

01

Customer Flow

Splash → Book a service → Track → Reschedule or rate
  1. 1
    Splash & OTP Login
  2. 2
    Search or Browse Categories
  3. 3
    Select Service, Date & Time
  4. 4
    Add Address & Job Details
  5. 5
    Confirm Booking
  6. 6
    Auto-Assignment Engine Matches a Tasker
  7. 7
    Track Booking
    Reschedule, cancel, or contact the tasker at any point
  8. 8
    Service Completed & Paid
  9. 9
    Rate & Save as Preferred Tasker
02

Tasker Flow

Login → Accept job → Work → Approval loop → Earnings
  1. 1
    OTP Login & Dashboard
  2. 2
    Review Today's Assigned Jobs
  3. 3
    Open Job Details
  4. ?
    Accept Job?
    If declined, the job is offered to another tasker
  5. 4
    Navigate & Start Job
  6. 5
    Complete Job — Add Summary & Photos
  7. ?
    Customer Approves?
    If changes are requested, the tasker updates and resubmits
  8. 6
    Job Closed — Earnings Updated
03

Admin Flow

New booking → Auto-match → Review → Track → Report
  1. 1
    Admin Login → Dashboard
  2. 2
    New Booking Received
  3. 3
    Check Tasker Availability
  4. 4
    Auto-Assignment Recommendation
  5. 5
    Review & Assign Tasker
    Manual override available whenever it's needed
  6. 6
    Tasker Notified → Job In Progress → Completed
  7. 7
    Customer Review Logged
  8. 8
    Reports & Analytics Updated

05 — Efficiency Fixes

Simple to use. Efficient underneath.

Six decisions that let the system stay this small on the surface without cutting corners on how it actually runs.

FIX-01
Auto-Assignment Engine

Every new booking is checked against real-time tasker availability and given a recommended match automatically — dispatch stops being a phone call and becomes a review step.

FIX-02
Self-Serve Reschedule

Customers can move a booking themselves up until a set cutoff, with a reason field for the tasker — no call center, no waiting on hold.

FIX-03
Preferred Taskers

A single toggle after a good job adds that tasker to a shortlist, so the next booking for the same trade can route straight to someone the customer already trusts.

FIX-04
Manual Override, Not Manual Default

The admin only touches an assignment when the algorithm's suggestion needs a human judgment call — the default path never waits on a person.

FIX-05
OTP-Only Login

No passwords to forget on either the customer or tasker app — one phone number, one code, into the app in seconds.

FIX-06
Photo Approval Loop

A tasker closes a job with a work summary and photos; the customer approves or requests changes before it counts as complete — trust without a back-office call.

Sample dataset shown in the Admin Dashboard design — illustrating the kind of load the assignment engine and reporting views are built to handle.

Total Bookings
36

198
